Arch-Builder
  • API Reference
  • View page source

API Reference

This section documents the main Python modules that drive build orchestration, configuration assembly, chroot execution, and ISO generation.

CLI Module

cli.main()[source]

Orchestration

Build Orchestrator - The Build Workflow Conductor

Coordinates all arch-builder components to run the complete ISO build process.

exception core.orchestrator.BuildOrchestratorError[source]

Bases: Exception

Exception raised for build orchestration failures.

class core.orchestrator.BuildOrchestrator(arch, config_path, mode='mock', clean=True, force_isolated_toolchain=False, toolchain_debug=False, toolchain_debug_log=None, toolchain_pacman_retries=3, desktop=None, kernel=None, bootloader=None, package_profiles=None, service_profiles=None, live_profile=None, live_user=None, live_groups=None)[source]

Bases: object

Coordinates the build workflow. It ties together configuration, the architecture engine, and the chroot environment.

Parameters:

  • arch (str)

  • config_path (str)

  • mode (str)

  • clean (bool)

  • force_isolated_toolchain (bool)

  • toolchain_debug (bool)

  • toolchain_debug_log (str | None)

  • toolchain_pacman_retries (int)

  • desktop (str | None)

  • kernel (str | None)

  • bootloader (str | None)

  • package_profiles (List[str] | None)

  • service_profiles (List[str] | None)

  • live_profile (str | None)

  • live_user (str | None)

  • live_groups (List[str] | None)

run_build(output_iso)[source]

Run the full build pipeline by delegating to the builder.

Parameters:

output_iso (str)

Return type:

Path

Configuration Loader

exception core.config_loader.ConfigValidationError[source]

Bases: Exception

Exception raised for configuration validation errors.

class core.config_loader.Config(data)[source]

Bases: object

Data wrapper for configuration objects with dot-notation access.

Parameters:

data (Dict[str, Any] | Config)

get(path, default=None)[source]
Parameters:

  • path (str)

  • default (Any)

Return type:

Any

to_dict()[source]
Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

class core.config_loader.ConfigAssembler(config_root)[source]

Bases: object

The assembler is the composition brain. It reads the global manifest and merges the configuration of all components (architectures, desktops, bootloaders, and so on) into a single configuration object.

Parameters:

config_root (str)

assemble(target_arch, target_desktop=None, target_kernel=None, target_bootloader=None, package_profiles=None, service_profiles=None, target_live_profile=None, live_user=None, live_groups=None)[source]

Configuration assembly process: 1. Load the global manifest (global_build.json). 2. Load the architecture-specific configuration. 3. Load the requested desktop profile. 4. Merge everything together.

Parameters:

  • target_arch (str)

  • target_desktop (str | None)

  • target_kernel (str | None)

  • target_bootloader (str | None)

  • package_profiles (List[str] | None)

  • service_profiles (List[str] | None)

  • target_live_profile (str | None)

  • live_user (str | None)

  • live_groups (List[str] | None)

Return type:

Config

class core.config_loader.ConfigLoader(config_root=None)[source]

Bases: object

Parameters:

config_root (str | None)

load_arch_config(global_path, arch)[source]
Parameters:

  • global_path (str)

  • arch (str)

Return type:

Dict[str, Any] | None

ISO Engine

core/iso_engine.py

Central build engine definitions. ISOBuilder is the canonical high-level build API. ArchBuilder remains as a backward-compatible alias.

class core.iso_engine.ArchBuilder(arch_name, config, toolchain=None)[source]

Bases: ISOBuilder

Backward-compatible alias for the historic builder name.

Parameters:

  • arch_name (str)

  • config (Config)

  • toolchain (Any | None)

class core.iso_engine.ArchEngine(arch, config, toolchain)[source]

Bases: BaseEngine

Engine in charge of x86_64 builds.

Parameters:

  • arch (str)

  • config (Config)

  • toolchain (Any)

setup_chroot(workdir)[source]

Prepare the chroot environment.

Parameters:

workdir (str)

Return type:

None

install_packages()[source]

Install target packages inside the chroot.

Return type:

None

build_bootloaders(mountpoint)[source]

Build the complete Arch Linux live ISO directory structure.

Standard Arch ISO layout:

/
├── boot/
│   ├── vmlinuz-linux       # Kernel
│   ├── initramfs-linux.img # Initramfs
│   └── grub/
│       ├── grub.cfg        # GRUB config
│       └── boot.img        # GRUB BIOS boot image
├── EFI/
│   └── BOOT/
│       └── BOOTx64.EFI     # GRUB EFI binary
├── loader/
│   ├── loader.conf         # systemd-boot config
│   └── entries/
│       └── arch-live.conf  # Boot entry
└── arch/
    └── x86_64/
        └── airootfs.sfs    # Squashfs root filesystem
Parameters:

mountpoint (str)

Return type:

None

finalize_isofile(output_path)[source]

Create the final bootable ISO using grub-mkrescue.

grub-mkrescue is the standard tool for creating bootable Arch Linux ISOs. It handles all the complexity of: - Creating proper El Torito boot images for BIOS - Creating EFI boot images for UEFI - Making the ISO hybrid (bootable from both BIOS and UEFI) - Embedding all required GRUB modules

In mock mode, this method only logs the intent without executing.

Parameters:

output_path (str)

Return type:

None

post_install_configure()[source]

Run post-install configuration steps.

Return type:

None

class core.iso_engine.BaseEngine(arch, config, toolchain)[source]

Bases: ISOEngine

Common engine behavior shared across all architecture-specific engines.

Parameters:

  • arch (str)

  • config (Config)

  • toolchain (Any)

setup_workdir(workdir=None)[source]
Parameters:

workdir (str | Path | None)

Return type:

Path

abstractmethod setup_chroot(workdir)[source]

Prepare the chroot environment.

Parameters:

workdir (str)

Return type:

None

abstractmethod install_packages()[source]

Install target packages inside the chroot.

Return type:

None

Generate bootloader artifacts for the target architecture.

Parameters:

mountpoint (str)

Return type:

None

abstractmethod post_install_configure()[source]

Run post-install configuration steps.

Return type:

None

abstractmethod finalize_isofile(output_path)[source]

Produce the final ISO file.

Parameters:

output_path (str)

Return type:

None

Canonical high-level build orchestrator used by the project.

Path

exception core.iso_engine.ISOBuilderError[source]

Bases: Exception

Raised when the build orchestration flow cannot proceed.

class core.iso_engine.ISOEngine[source]

Bases: ABC

Abstract base for architecture-specific build engines.

classmethod register(arch_name)[source]

Register a virtual subclass of an ABC.

Returns the subclass, to allow usage as a class decorator.

Parameters:

arch_name (str)

Chroot Manager

core/chroot_manager.py

Centralised management of the chroot environment (mock or real).

This module provides: * Mock filesystem handling for unit‑testing and development. * A ChrootManager that can switch between mock and real modes. * Integration with the central logging system (core.logger_setup).

exception core.chroot_manager.ChrootManagerError[source]

Bases: Exception

Raised when chroot lifecycle or command execution fails.

exception core.chroot_manager.ChrootError[source]

Bases: ChrootManagerError

Backward-compatible alias for older imports.

class core.chroot_manager.MockFSHandler(base_dir)[source]

Bases: object

Simulates a minimal filesystem inside the chroot directory.

Parameters:

base_dir (str)

create_file(path, content)[source]

Simulate creating a file – logs the operation.

Parameters:

  • path (str)

  • content (str)

Return type:

None

read_file(path)[source]

Simulate reading a file – logs the operation.

Parameters:

path (str)

Return type:

str | None

list_directory(path)[source]

Simulate directory listing – used by tests.

Parameters:

path (str)

Return type:

List[str]

class core.chroot_manager.ChrootManager(workdir=None, chroot_path=None, mode='mock', chroot_mode=None, toolchain=None, arch=None)[source]

Bases: object

Manages a chroot environment that can operate in two modes: * Mock mode – pure Python simulation (no external processes). * Real mode – spawns subprocesses with sudo/chroot (requires privileges).

Parameters:

  • workdir (str | None)

  • chroot_path (Path | None)

  • mode (str)

  • chroot_mode (bool | None)

  • toolchain (Any | None)

  • arch (str | None)

set_real_mode()[source]

Switch from mock to real execution – requires sudo etc.

Return type:

None

setup()[source]

Prepare the basic directory structure of the chroot.

Return type:

str

cleanup()[source]

Compatibility no-op used by the orchestrator in mock mode.

Return type:

None

run_command(command, chroot_path=None)[source]

Execute a command either in mock mode (simple logging) or real mode (spawn a subprocess inside the chroot). Returns stdout.

Parameters:

  • command (List[str] | str)

  • chroot_path (str | None)

Return type:

str

install_packages(packages)[source]

Install official, local and AUR packages inside the chroot (mock or real).

Parameters:

packages (List[str] | Dict[str, Any])

Return type:

None

generate_fstab()[source]

Generate a simple /etc/fstab and persist it inside the chroot.

Return type:

str

Customizer

System Configurator - System Customization and Configuration

Responsible for applying customization rules that include: 1. Copying files into the filesystem. 2. Running system commands (for example, systemctl enable).

exception core.customizer.ConfigError[source]

Bases: Exception

Exception raised for system configuration errors.

class core.customizer.SystemAction[source]

Bases: object

Base class for a configuration action.

execute(chroot, source_base)[source]
Parameters:

  • chroot (ChrootManager)

  • source_base (Path)

class core.customizer.OverlayAction(overlay_dir)[source]

Bases: SystemAction

Copy a full overlay directory onto the chroot (like airootfs).

Parameters:

overlay_dir (str)

execute(chroot, source_base)[source]
Parameters:

  • chroot (ChrootManager)

  • source_base (Path)

class core.customizer.FileAction(src, dest, mode=None)[source]

Bases: SystemAction

Copy one file from a source path to a destination inside the chroot.

Parameters:

  • src (str)

  • dest (str)

  • mode (str | None)

execute(chroot, source_base)[source]
Parameters:

  • chroot (ChrootManager)

  • source_base (Path)

class core.customizer.CommandAction(command)[source]

Bases: SystemAction

Execute a command directly inside the chroot.

Parameters:

command (str)

execute(chroot, source_base)[source]
Parameters:

  • chroot (ChrootManager)

  • source_base (Path)

class core.customizer.UserAction(user_config)[source]

Bases: SystemAction

Create users and apply their related settings.

Parameters:

user_config (Dict[str, Any])

execute(chroot, source_base)[source]
Parameters:

  • chroot (ChrootManager)

  • source_base (Path)

class core.customizer.ServiceAction(services)[source]

Bases: SystemAction

Enable systemd services.

Parameters:

services (List[str])

execute(chroot, source_base)[source]
Parameters:

  • chroot (ChrootManager)

  • source_base (Path)

class core.customizer.MkinitcpioAction(initramfs_config)[source]

Bases: SystemAction

Configure initramfs generation through mkinitcpio.

Parameters:

initramfs_config (Dict[str, Any])

execute(chroot, source_base)[source]
Parameters:

  • chroot (ChrootManager)

  • source_base (Path)

class core.customizer.LocaleAction(config)[source]

Bases: SystemAction

Configure locales, timezone, hostname, and keymap.

Parameters:

config (Dict[str, Any])

execute(chroot, source_base)[source]
Parameters:

  • chroot (ChrootManager)

  • source_base (Path)

class core.customizer.SystemConfigurator(chroot=None)[source]

Bases: object

The configurator is the system customization coordinator. It processes configuration “customizations” and applies them to the environment.

Parameters:

chroot (ChrootManager | None)

load_from_config(config)[source]

Load actions from the Config object, which already contains merged data from global_build.json, architecture.json, and other sources.

Parameters:

config (Any)

apply(source_base_dir=None)[source]

Apply all registered actions to the chroot.

Parameters:

source_base_dir (Path | None)

Toolchain

Toolchain Manager (Build Environment)

Manages a secondary chroot environment where build tools (pacman, mksquashfs, xorriso, mtools) are executed. This avoids polluting the host operating system and helps ensure reproducibility across any Linux distribution.

class core.toolchain.ToolchainManager(workdir_base, mode='mock', force_isolated=False, pacman_retries=3, diagnostics_enabled=False, diagnostics_log_path=None, pacman_cache_dir=None, arch=None)[source]

Bases: object

Parameters:

  • workdir_base (Path)

  • mode (str)

  • force_isolated (bool)

  • pacman_retries (int)

  • diagnostics_enabled (bool)

  • diagnostics_log_path (Path | None)

  • pacman_cache_dir (Path | None)

  • arch (str | None)

setup()[source]

Ensure the tool environment is ready. In a real scenario without the required host tools, this would download an archlinux-bootstrap.tar.gz archive and extract it into self.toolchain_dir.

run_tool(command, cwd=None)[source]

Execute a build tool. If the tools are available on the host, run the command directly. Otherwise, execute it inside the isolated build chroot.

Parameters:

  • command (list)

  • cwd (Path)

Return type:

CompletedProcess

run_command(command, chroot_path=None)[source]

Compatibility wrapper expected by ISOBuilder/BaseEngine.

Parameters:

  • command (list)

  • chroot_path (str)

Return type:

str

execute_command(command, chroot_path=None)[source]

Backward-compatible command runner alias.

Parameters:

  • command (list)

  • chroot_path (str)

Return type:

str

cleanup()[source]

Release mounts created for isolated build hosts.

Return type:

None
